title: Add --registry-token flags to support Bearer token authentication
body:
This feature https://github.com/containers/image/pull/842 was merged in containers/image to support authentication using a provided Bearer token (example use case: integration with Harbor webhooks).

This PR adds a flag to the skopeo CLI to provide support for the registry Bearer token.

Signed-off-by: Alvaro Iradier <airadier@gmail.com>

comments.body: Working on the unittests, expect a new commit soon.
OK, maybe I need more tea, but I see the `--src-registry-token` and `--dest-registry-token` in the bash completion and tests, but not the code?  Am I being oblivious (again)?

> OK, maybe I need more tea, but I see the `--src-registry-token` and `--dest-registry-token` in the bash completion and tests, but not the code? Am I being oblivious (again)?

I also noticed this, but the existing unit tests don't check anything with the "src" prefix.

In the implementation of utils.go, the flag parsing can be done without or with a prefix ("src" or "dest" currently), so I guess that testing flag parsing with prefix "whatever" and then testing the parameters `--whatever-registry-token` is enough. The same applies for `--whatever-cert-dir`, `--whatever-creds`, etc.

However, for the bash-completion we have to include both "src" and "dest" and the no prefix case.

> In the implementation of `utils.go`, the flag parsing can be done without or with a prefix ("src" or "dest" currently), so I guess that testing flag parsing with prefix "whatever" and then testing the parameters `--whatever-registry-token` is enough. 

Yes, that’s the idea. “whatever” just happens to be spelled `dest` in the tests.
